title: Merge the completed persona-action full-support remediation branch into development
execution_plan_ref: Operations/EXECUTION_PLAN.md § Initiative: M365 Persona-Action Full-Support Merge to Development

principles: truthful integration of the already governed M365-only persona/action workforce into development
fail-closed merge governance with bounded branch scope
anti_alignment: Does NOT promote to staging or main.
Does NOT reopen P1 through P5 semantics during merge execution.
Does NOT widen into UCP or non-M365 runtime work.

user_stated_requirements: are we on a feature branch and can it be merged into development
prepare that formal merge package next
do not merge yet; prepare the governed package first
intent_capture.intent_verification: R1 through R6 capture the bounded merge-to-development work required before any actual branch mutation.

objective: Merge the completed persona-action full-support remediation surface from codex/m365-persona-action-full-support-remediation into development via an explicit merge commit and stop before any staging or main promotion.

final_truth_to_preserve: 103 green / 36 approval-gated / 1 actor-tier-gated / 32 fenced unique aliases
360 green / 49 approval-gated / 1 actor-tier-gated / 35 fenced active pairs

options_considered.description: Create a governed merge-to-development package and execute the merge only after approval.
pros: Produces a clear rollback and validation path.
cons: Adds one extra governance step before the actual merge.

options_considered.description: Merge directly without a new plan.
Fold the merge into a staging and main promotion package immediately.
pros: Collapses later promotion work into one package.
cons: Widens scope beyond the requested development merge.

complexity_warning.statement: Combining development merge, staging/main promotion, and release publication in one package adds release-surface risk without improving deterministic merge governance.
complexity_warning.simpler_path: Create one bounded merge-to-development package first and stop after development is green and pushed.

in_scope: verify source and target branch readiness
inspect branch drift between the source branch and development
merge the completed source branch into development with an explicit merge commit
resolve only bounded conflicts inside the already governed P1-P5 remediation surface and governance trackers
rerun the final persona-action remediation validation slice on merged development
update the merge package and governance trackers truthfully
push development only after green validation
out_of_scope: new persona, capability, or workforce-model changes
file_allowlist: any file already changed in the governed P1-P5 remediation surface between 8dfa986 and ce652dc, but only when required for bounded conflict resolution that preserves the proven P1-P5 semantics
file_denylist: any file outside the bounded source-branch diff unless separately governed
scope.scope_fence_rule: Stop and open a new governed scope-correction package if origin/development drift or merge conflicts require semantic edits outside the bounded source-branch diff or tracker surfaces.

requirements.description: Verify source and target branch readiness before mutating development.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Source branch is clean and pushed, development is fetched and clean, and branch inventory is captured.
requirements.user_intent_trace: prepare the merge package for this completed branch

requirements.description: Keep the merge bounded to development only.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: No staging, main, or release-tag action occurs during execution.

requirements.description: Merge the governed source branch into development with an explicit merge commit and bounded conflict resolution.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Development contains a merge result from the source branch, and any conflict resolution stays inside the bounded merge surface.
requirements.user_intent_trace: merge this completed branch into development

requirements.description: Re-run the final persona-action remediation validation slice on the merged development state.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The focused verifier and pytest slices, pre-commit, and git diff --check are green on merged development.
requirements.user_intent_trace: validate the merge result before push

requirements.description: Synchronize trackers and package status truthfully.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Operations trackers plus this package reflect the actual merge outcome and next act.

requirements.description: Push development only after green validation and stop before any further promotion.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: origin/development matches the validated merge result, and staging/main remain untouched.

tasks.name: Preflight inventory
tasks.description: Capture source and target branch readiness, drift, and bounded merge surface before any mutation.
exit_criteria: The source branch is still clean and pushed.
The bounded merge surface is explicit.
tasks.validation_command: git status --short --branch && git rev-parse codex/m365-persona-action-full-support-remediation development origin/development

tasks.name: Approval packet and governance gate
tasks.description: Present the merge approval packet and validate every mutating action before execution.
tasks.validation_command: echo "approval packet presented before merge execution"

tasks.name: Merge into development
tasks.description: Merge the governed source branch into development with an explicit merge commit and bounded conflict resolution.
exit_criteria: Development contains the source-branch merge result.
No conflict resolution escapes the bounded merge surface.

tasks.name: Validate merged development
tasks.description: Run the final persona-action remediation validation slice on the merged development state.
deliverables: green verifier, test, pre-commit, and diff-check results
exit_criteria: The final governed 172-alias / 445-pair truth still holds on development.

tasks.name: Push development and close governance
tasks.description: Update trackers, push development, and freeze the merge result.
exit_criteria: origin/development matches the validated merge result.
This package and the trackers reflect the actual result.

commands: python3 -m py_compile src/ops_adapter/actions.py src/smarthaus_common/permission_enforcer.py src/smarthaus_common/executor_routing.py src/smarthaus_common/approval_risk.py tests/test_ops_adapter.py tests/test_policies.py tests/test_persona_registry_v2.py tests/test_operations_department_pack_v1.py tests/test_project_management_department_pack_v1.py tests/test_engineering_department_pack_v1.py
validation_strategy.replay_branch_inspection: Require local development and origin/development to resolve to the same merge result after push.

agent_constraints: Do not merge to staging or main in this package.
Do not silently rewrite P1 through P5 semantics during conflict resolution.
Stop if origin/development drift changes the merge surface materially.

summary: R1 begins only after the approval packet is presented and the merge package is the active next act.
No merge, development mutation, or promotion work has been executed in this package-creation slice.
